## [00:00 - 03:00]

Okay, so now I want to talk about your sales video, or it could be a sales letter, but we're mostly going to talk about sales video. And there's one absolute thing that you must do, and this is the most important thing. This is probably the biggest mistake I see in sales letters and sales videos. But you know, so I'm going to walk you through the whole thing. We're going to give you step-by-step on how to do this. But the most important thing, if you get this right, then it's like game over, you win, is you need to focus on the transformation. And what I mean by that is your prospect, when they stop up and purchase from you, then their life is going to change in some way. That's why you're selling them something. And there's going to be some transformation in their life. If you don't like that word, think about change. How is their life going to change? What is the impact they're going to be on their life once they purchase your product? It's all about the transformation. It's not about the stuff. It's not about the whatever they get, the goop or the DVDs or the CDs or the widget or whatever. It's about how their life's actually going to change because that's really what they want. If you look at product launch formula, when I sold this product, whenever I sell product launch formula, I don't talk about you're going to get a bunch of videos, you're going to get a bunch of modules, you're going to get this, you're going to get that. I talk about how it's going to change their life. They're going to be able to launch a business. They're going to be able to launch a product and have that instant momentum. They're not going to be dependent on hope marketing. That's what I mean about the transformation. If you're teaching people how to play songs on a guitar, it's not that you're going to send them a book and a DVD. You're going to give them the ability to be able to play songs for themselves and for their friends. Their ability to entertain people, to be the center of a party. The self-worth that they're going to have from we've been able to play songs. If you're going to teach them how to train their dog, again, it's not some DVDs, it's not some videos, it's not a forum. It's having this dog that will come instantly when you call them every time and the greater joy that they'll have in their life because of their better relationship with their dog. If you're going to teach them how to play tennis, it's again, it's not about what the actual stuff is, it's the ability to walk on the court and have the confidence and win that match. The stuff doesn't matter. Or it might matter a little bit, but it only matters 10%. You've got to focus on the transformation. So if you forget everything else, but you get this right, then you're going to end up with a great sales message, a great sales video. Another thing is, don't assume that everyone who's going to see your sales video has seen your pre-launch content. That they've been actually through your launch. In an ideal world, they will have watched all your pieces of pre-launch content. They'll have watched them twice.

## [03:00 - 06:01]

They'll have memorized them. But the reality is people are busy and they don't always watch every single one of your pre-launch videos. They don't, or they might not remember them. Or they'll skip through them, or they'll see a beginning and they'll turn it off. So you can't assume, don't go into the sales letter or the sales video and just start making the sale. You have to open with the big promise. Now if you go back to your first piece of pre-launch content, hopefully you open that with a big promise. And you grab their attention. You have to do the same thing with your sales video. You don't just come into the sales video and say, well, you saw my pre-launch content, this is great stuff and this is what it cost. You have to basically, you have to grab their attention and you have to revisit like that overall, that promise and the overall launch theme. It doesn't mean you're going to completely repeat your pre-launch content, right? This isn't like, you did three 18 minute videos and now you're gonna just repeat them and then ask for the money. This is a sales video. I'm gonna walk you through the process. But you can't assume they've seen that promise. They've seen that theme that you had in your pre-launch. You don't wanna get overly complicated. It's really easy to get, just get way too complex with these things. And you don't wanna be too boring. In fact, you don't wanna be boring at all. And in terms of length, a lot of times people worry about the length, how long should it be? And this might sound a little simplistic, but you need to make it as long as it needs to be and not one second longer. So you need to tell your whole story. The whole story of your product. You need to tell that promise and the story of your product and why it's gonna change their lives and how it's gonna transform their lives. So you gotta make it as long as it needs to be and not any longer. Don't be boring. if your spouse is not a golfer. It has to be interesting to the people that you're selling to. ## [09:02 - 09:18]

Not from your heels, you wanna be selling from certainty and from power. And you do that, you get there by focusing on that transformation that your product is going to have. Make it real in your mind and then you're gonna be able to step in front of the camera and just knock it out of the park.
